title: The New Family Story Written into the Wall
subtitle: A visualisation for seeing the new money story as something already solid
excerpt: A ten-minute visualisation for Day 14. The scene places the new family money story as a physical inscription — a sentence carved into the wall of a house you own. The image holds the permanence of what you are starting.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 14 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796): celebrating the new family money story.

practice.whenToUse: On Day 14 of the money arc, or when you want an image that makes the new family story feel real and permanent rather than aspirational.

content.text: A visualisation for Day 14. The scene is a house you own. The image gives the new family money story a physical location — words in a wall, permanent, already there. Sit comfortably, close your eyes, and let the image build at its own pace.
You are standing in a hallway. It's a house you own — the one you have now, or one you are building toward. The light is ordinary. The floor is solid under your feet.

Look at the wall in front of you. There is a sentence carved or painted there — not a framed print, not a quote from somewhere else, but something written into the structure of the wall itself. The new family money story, in a few words.

Take a moment to read it. It says something about ease, or abundance, or the way money works in this family now. It might say "In this family, money flows easily and stays." It might say something you didn't expect. Whatever appears, let it be there.

Walk through the house slowly. One room at a time. Notice how money is present here — the things you bought without panic, the savings that are building, the way you don't check the account before buying something ordinary. The ease isn't dramatic; it's just quiet.
Come back to the hallway. Read the wall again. This is the story you are writing — with your choices, your practices, the work you are doing.
When you're ready, open your eyes and write one sentence: what the wall said.
Original to homemade.education. Built from the Day 14 theme of
(Rebecca J Page, 2025): celebrating the new family story and the turning point already in motion.
